title: Phyllorkis josephi
taxonomy: Phyllorkis josephi is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia. The genus Phyllorkis is a synonym for the genus Pinalia, which is a group of orchids that are often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Pinalia is a genus within the subtribe Aeridinae, which includes many species of orchids that are often terrestrial and have a wide range of flower colors. The classification of Phyllorkis josephi reflects its evolutionary relationships with other orchids and its shared characteristics with other species in the family Orchidaceae.
